title: De ce continuă Putin în Ucraina un război pe care deja l-a pierdut?
summary: Finnish President Alexander Stubb argues at Davos that Putin has already strategically lost the war in Ukraine, having failed to absorb Ukraine, inadvertently expanded NATO, and triggered European remilitarization. Putin continues the war because it has become 'too big to fail' — stopping without a victory would collapse his myth of power and destabilize his internal control.
talking_points: Putin has failed all strategic objectives: Ukraine is moving toward EU membership, NATO expanded with Finland and Sweden, and Europe is remilitarizing
Russia's territorial gains over the past two years are minimal (under 1%) while human costs are enormous (34,000 soldiers killed in December alone)
The war has become 'too big to fail' for Putin — ending it without victory would threaten his internal power and expose economic collapse
Stubb frames this as a world-order-defining moment comparable to 1918, 1945, or 1989, calling for 'values-based realism'
Ukraine represents the front line of defense for the entire free world and the liberal order
concepts: too big to fail doctrine applied to war
author: Redacția
